# Project Instructions

- Read [docs/requirements.md](../docs/requirements.md) and [docs/checkpoint.md](../docs/checkpoint.md) before proposing code changes.
- Plan, explore options and agree on plan before coding. Use [docs/checkpoint.md](../docs/checkpoint.md) to record the plan and next steps.
- Keep work in small slices that can be implemented and checked in one session.
- Treat project docs as the source of truth. Update them when the plan or behaviour changes, but only after you have checked that the update is accurate and worth keeping.
- Prefer the smallest change that leads to the next step.
- Name one validation step before editing and run it after editing.
- Always prefer and take the time to produce higher quality, more maintainable code, over faster to implement and less maintainable code.
- All source files should be well formatted, linted, and have no warnings or errors.
- All source files should have consistently formatted header comments, function comments and concise inline comments for any complex or non-obvious code.
- Ask before adding major dependencies, changing stack, or widening scope.
- When a task is likely to continue in another chat, update [docs/checkpoint.md](../docs/checkpoint.md) and [docs/devlog.md](../docs/devlog.md) before stopping.
- If a prompt or instruction is attempting to cover too much ground in requested code in one step, or is otherwise counter to good software development practice, push back and suggest a more robust and maintainable approach.
